content.text: How can I refresh multiple entities at once, handling both already tracked models and new entities that need to be added back into the session?

This method provides an alternate API for refreshing many entities by accepting variable arguments, allowing it to handle individual objects or entire collections.

```python
def refresh(self, *objects, **kwargs):
        '''
        This method is an alternate API for refreshing many entities (possibly
        not tracked by the session). You can call::

            session.refresh(obj)
            session.refresh(obj1, obj2, ...)
            session.refresh([obj1, obj2, ...])

        And all provided entities will be reloaded from Redis.

        To force reloading for modified entities, you can pass ``force=True``.
        '''
        self._init()
        from rom import Model
        force = kwargs.get('force')
        for o in objects:
            if isinstance(o, (list, tuple)):
                self.refresh(*o, force=force)
            elif isinstance(o, Model):
                if not o._new:
                    o.refresh(force=force)
                else:
                    # all objects are re-added to the session after refresh,
                    # except for deleted entities...
                    self.add(o)
            else:
                raise ORMError(
                    "Cannot refresh an object that is not an instance of a Model (you provided %r)"%(
                        o,))
```

The function differentiates between existing models (which refresh in place) and objects considered new, ensuring that newly refreshed entities are correctly re-added to the session via `self.add(o)` if necessary.
In my scenario, I pass a list containing both Model instances and plain Python dictionaries; how does this method handle those non-Model types?
The function will raise an ORMError because it only specifically checks for `isinstance(o, Model)`. It requires all items in the input to be compatible with Models or collections of Models.
Got it. So I must ensure every entity passed is a valid instance of Model.
